description:
You are a machine-learning engineer cleaning up a tiny tab-separated
    training file for an experiment.

    1.  A source file already exists at  
        /home/user/data/raw_train.tsv

        •  It contains **four columns** in the exact order  
           label<TAB>sepal_length<TAB>sepal_width<TAB>petal_length.  
        •  The very first line is a header row that uses those same
           column names.  
        •  There are exactly **three** data rows after the header.  
        •  All fields are separated by one (and only one) TAB character,
           and every line (including the last) ends with a UNIX newline
           character (`\n`).  

    2.  Down-stream code expects the three numeric feature columns to
        appear first and the label to appear last.

    Your job:

        a.  Re-order the columns with the help of the standard
            GNU coreutils **cut** and **paste** commands
            (2–3 simple commands should be enough; no Python, no awk).  
        b.  Produce a new file  
            /home/user/data/processed_train.tsv  
            that contains the columns in this exact order:

                sepal_length<TAB>sepal_width<TAB>petal_length<TAB>label

            Preserve every data row, preserve the original numeric
            precision, and do not introduce extra whitespace.

        c.  Write a two-line verification log
            /home/user/data/processing.log  
            with the following exact content and formatting:

                raw_lines=4
                processed_lines=4

            (Each line followed by a single newline character.  
             No additional spaces or blank lines.)

    The automated checker will confirm **all** of the following:

        •  /home/user/data/processed_train.tsv exists.  
        •  It contains exactly four TAB-separated columns in the
           specified order and exactly four lines (1 header + 3 data).  
        •  The text inside /home/user/data/processing.log is
           exactly as shown above.  
        •  No stray temporary files remain in /home/user/data.  

    You do not have root privileges, so work entirely inside /home/user.
    Temporary helper files are fine while you work, but they should be
    gone (or at least outside /home/user/data) once you are done.
truth:
Initial state (already present before the agent starts):

    •  Directory: /home/user/data  (rwx for user)
    •  File: /home/user/data/raw_train.tsv  
       Contents (TABs shown here as \t for clarity; real file contains
       actual TAB characters):

           label\tsepal_length\tsepal_width\tpetal_length\n
           Iris-setosa\t5.1\t3.5\t1.4\n
           Iris-versicolor\t7.0\t3.2\t4.7\n
           Iris-virginica\t6.3\t3.3\t6.0\n

    Expected state after the agent finishes:

    1. File: /home/user/data/processed_train.tsv  
       Expected contents (again, real TAB characters, each line ending
       in \n):

           sepal_length\tsepal_width\tpetal_length\tlabel\n
           5.1\t3.5\t1.4\tIris-setosa\n
           7.0\t3.2\t4.7\tIris-versicolor\n
           6.3\t3.3\t6.0\tIris-virginica\n

    2. File: /home/user/data/processing.log  
       Exact contents:

           raw_lines=4\n
           processed_lines=4\n

    3. No other files remain in /home/user/data except:
           raw_train.tsv
           processed_train.tsv
           processing.log
